question: Is the subject depicted as an elderly woman with short gray hair and thick black-framed glasses, matching the subject reference?
Is the specific mole on the woman's left cheek clearly visible?
Is the woman shown performing the action of knitting with needles and yarn?
Is the image rendered primarily as a black and white woodcut print with bold, high-contrast shapes?
Are shadows and volume rendered using hatching or cross-hatching lines rather than smooth digital gradients?
Is the green yarn the sole accent color in the image, with all other elements strictly in black and white?
Do the edges exhibit the slight roughness characteristic of woodcut carving while remaining sharp and free of blur?
Is the figure rotated to a three-quarter profile view, consistent with the angle of the structure reference?
Are the hands and knitting needles positioned centrally in the frame?
Are the desk lamp (left) and bookshelf (background) positioned consistent with the layout of the structure reference?
Are the hands and fingers rendered with plausible anatomy (e.g., no missing/extra fingers or fused joints)?
Are the knitting needles rendered as straight, rigid tools, without warping or blending into the hands?
Is the image free from significant visual artifacts, digital noise, or unintentional blurring?
